title: theme-atoms v1 theme
description: A self-contained shell/terminal theme. Consumed by aish v0.2 via a single HTTP GET; no second fetch required for brand-atoms or any external reference.

schema.description: Pin the schema URI so consumers can verify they understand this version.

id.description: URL-safe slug; also the filename stem (id.toml).

version.description: Semver. Theme content changes bump version.

extends_brand.description: Documentary reference to a brand-atoms brand. NOT a fetch target — palette below MUST be inlined.

palette.description: Inlined palette. Keys are role names (e.g., fg, bg, green, blue, accent). Single-fetch contract: do not reference external palettes.

segments.description: Ordered list of segments to render. Unknown segments MUST be rejected by the consumer. See docs/elements.md for the data contract.

glyphs.description: Glyph set. nerd-default requires a Nerd Font.

roles.description: Semantic role → color mappings. Consumers look up runtime roles here (e.g., ai_tier_local).

syntax.description: Syntax-highlighting token → color mappings. Optional; consumers fall back to roles or defaults.

font.description: Preferred font for rendering this theme. A strong preference: aish-term / TUI consumers SHOULD match this font when available; plain CLI consumers MAY ignore. Users always retain a local override.

family.description: Primary font family. Should be a monospace font for prompt + segments to line up.

fallback.description: Ordered fallback chain. Consumer walks this list when the primary family is unavailable.

size.description: Point size (pt) hint. Consumer may scale.

ligatures.description: Enable programming ligatures (=> != etc). Requires a font that ships them.

nerd_font.description: Asserts the primary family is a Nerd Font patch. Required to be true when prompt.glyphs == 'nerd-default'.

layout.description: Compass layout: north (top), south (bottom), east (right), west (left) bars surround the always-present middle (the interactive terminal). Each bar holds named elements packed by alignment. Consumers render the bars they can; the middle is always rendered.

colorRef.description: Either a hex color (#RGB / #RRGGBB / #RRGGBBAA) or a palette reference {palette.NAME} that MUST resolve within this theme's own palette block. No external references.

elementId.description: Element ID. Consumers map this to a renderer (data + glyph). Unknown elements MAY be skipped silently. See docs/elements.md for the full catalog and data contracts.

elementStyle.description: Per-element style override. Both fields optional; absent means 'use consumer default'.

icon.description: Glyph (or short string) to render in place of the element's default icon. Empty string disables the icon.

color.description: Text color override for this element (otherwise the bar's foreground applies).

elementsMap.description: Per-element style overrides keyed by element id. Lets a theme decide each element's icon + text color independently of the bar's bg/fg.

horizontalBar.description: A north or south bar. Elements pack into three alignment buckets.

verticalBar.description: An east or west bar. Renders as a vertical panel of given width. Elements pack into three alignment buckets.
